steps.title: Recover the source equation
steps.content:
The question references "the given equation" relating \(p\), \(x\), and \(y\). Based on the answer choices and the correct result \(y = px + 34\), the source equation is most likely:

$$\frac{y - 34}{x} = p$$

This states that \(p\) equals the quantity \(y - 34\) divided by \(x\).

steps.title: Isolate y algebraically
steps.content:
Multiply both sides by \(x\):
$$y - 34 = px$$

Add 34 to both sides:
$$y = px + 34$$

This is choice B.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• A (\(y = 34x + p\)): Rearranging gives \(\frac{y-p}{x} = 34\), not \(p\). Eliminated.
  • C (\(y = 34px\)): Rearranging gives \(\frac{y}{34x} = p\), not \(\frac{y-34}{x}\). Eliminated.
  • D (\(y = \frac{px}{34}\)): Rearranging gives \(\frac{34y}{x} = p\), not \(\frac{y-34}{x}\). Eliminated.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
